Automatically validates JSON Web Tokens (JWT) for every incoming request (excluding public routes) . It performs role-based access control (e.g., restricting /api/parent/** to "parent" roles) and handles token revocation upon logout or password change

A background service that connects to an SMTP server to deliver account activation emails, OTP codes, or password reset links within ≤60 seconds of a request. It manages the OTP lifecycle, currently using in-memory storage with a recommendation to migrate to Redis for persistence.
Triggered upon video upload, this service encodes MP4 files into HLS (HTTP Live Streaming) format with multiple bitrates to optimize delivery via CDN for varied internet speeds

Generates temporary, time-limited Signed URLs for private assets stored in S3-compatible buckets (e.g., 1-hour TTL for lecture videos, 5-minute TTL for downloadable materials) to prevent unauthorized access
Processes automated callbacks from VNPay/MoMo. It verifies digital signatures and ensures idempotency to prevent duplicate enrollments if the same payment notification is received multiple times

Upon successful payment confirmation, the system automatically calculates the platform fee and teacher's share based on contract ratios, recording immutable snapshots in the revenue_splits table for future audits
Facilitates communication between the backend and the internal AI Engine via REST API, sending student context (progress, quiz scores) to retrieve personalized roadmaps or chatbot responses
A scheduled Cron job that performs daily snapshots of the PostgreSQL database and stores them in Cloud Storage for 30 days to ensure a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of ≤4 hours
Systematically records critical actions (e.g., course approval, account locking, quiz submissions) into centralized logs (ELK/Loki) with unique request_id and user_id tags for traceability

A scheduled background task that monitors and updates the status of expired links, such as activation links (24-hour limit) and parent-student link requests (7-day limit), moving them to a "REVOKED" or "EXPIRED" state

AS-1: Systems with appropriate user interfaces will be available for cafeteria employees to process the expected volume of meals ordered.
AS-2: Cafeteria staff and vehicles will be available to deliver all meals for specified delivery time slots within 15 minutes of the requested delivery time.
DE-1: If a restaurant has its own on-line ordering system, the Cafeteria Ordering System must be able to communicate with it bi-directionally.

[Provide common business rules that you must follow. The information can be provided in the table format as the sample below]

cells: Delivery time windows are 15 minutes, beginning on each quarter hour.
Deliveries must be completed between 10:00 A.M. and 2:00 P.M. local time, inclusive.
All meals in a single order must be delivered to the same location.
All meals in a single order must be paid for by using the same payment method.
If an order is to be delivered, the patron must pay by payroll deduction.

Order price is calculated as the sum of each food item price times the quantity of that food item ordered, plus applicable sales tax, plus a delivery charge if a meal is delivered outside the free delivery zone.
